description: Complete Autopsy workflow and feature reference from basic to advanced digital forensics analysis

commands.example: Launch Autopsy and select 'New Case' from the welcome screen
commands.explanation: Initializes the case database and directory structure for a new investigation

commands.example: Enter Case Name and Base Directory path
commands.explanation: Sets the identifier and storage location for all case data

commands.example: Input Examiner Name, Phone, and Email optional fields
commands.explanation: Associates the investigator's metadata with the case for reporting purposes

commands.command: Select Data Source Type - Disk Image
commands.example: Choose 'Disk Image or VM File' in the Add Data Source wizard
commands.explanation: Prepares Autopsy to ingest physical or virtual disk image files like E01 or VMDK

commands.command: Select Data Source Type - Logical Files
commands.example: Choose 'Logical Files' to analyze a specific folder without an image
commands.explanation: Ingests a live directory or specific files from the host system for analysis

commands.command: Select Data Source Type - Unallocated Space
commands.example: Choose 'Unallocated Space Image File' for raw binary chunks
commands.explanation: Ingests raw data chunks specifically for carving without filesystem structures

commands.example: Set the time zone to the suspect's local time or UTC
commands.explanation: Ensures accurate timestamp interpretation during file system processing

commands.example: Check 'Recent Activity' during ingest configuration
commands.explanation: Extracts user activity from registry, recent docs, and installed programs

commands.example: Check 'Hash Lookup' and select a hash set like NSRL
commands.explanation: Identifies known good files to ignore and known bad files to flag

commands.example: Check 'File Type Identification' to analyze file signatures
commands.explanation: Determines file types based on magic bytes rather than file extensions

commands.example: Check 'Embedded File Extractor' to open ZIP and RAR files
commands.explanation: Unpacks compressed archives to analyze the files contained within them

commands.example: Check 'Picture Analyzer' or Exif parser to extract metadata
commands.explanation: Extracts geolocation and camera device data from image files

commands.example: Check 'Keyword Search' to index text content
commands.explanation: Indexes file content to allow for fast text searching later in the investigation

commands.example: Check 'Email Parser' to process MBOX, PST, and OST files
commands.explanation: Extracts individual emails and attachments from mailbox container files

commands.example: Check 'Encryption Detection' to find password-protected files
commands.explanation: Flags files that are encrypted or password protected for manual review

commands.example: Check 'Interesting Files Identifier' using a custom rule set
commands.explanation: Flags files matching specific names or paths relevant to the investigation

commands.example: Expand 'Data Sources' > 'Image Name' > 'Vol' to browse folders
commands.explanation: Allows manual exploration of the file system hierarchy

commands.example: Select the 'Text' tab in the content viewer pane
commands.explanation: Displays the ASCII or Unicode text representation of the selected file

commands.example: Select the 'Hex' tab to view raw binary data
commands.explanation: Shows the raw hexadecimal and ASCII decoding of the file's bytes

commands.example: Select the 'Application' tab to render images or HTML
commands.explanation: Renders supported file types like images and HTML in their native format

commands.example: Select the 'File Metadata' tab to see timestamps and attributes
commands.explanation: Displays MAC times, permissions, and file system attributes

commands.example: Navigate to 'Web History' under 'Results' in the tree
commands.explanation: Lists visited URLs extracted from browser databases

commands.example: Navigate to 'Web Cookies' to see session data
commands.explanation: Displays tracking cookies and session identifiers from web browsers

commands.example: Navigate to 'Web Downloads' to find downloaded files
commands.explanation: Lists files downloaded via browsers including source URL and path

commands.example: Check 'Installed Programs' artifact category
commands.explanation: Lists software applications installed on the system via Registry or packages

commands.example: Check 'Operating System Information' artifact
commands.explanation: Shows OS version, install date, and system owner details

commands.example: Navigate to 'USB Device Attached' artifacts
commands.explanation: Lists external storage devices previously connected to the system

commands.example: Navigate to 'OS Accounts' artifact
commands.explanation: Lists user profiles, last login times, and account privileges

commands.example: Navigate to 'Views' > 'File Types' > 'By Extension'
commands.explanation: Groups files by their extension for quick review of specific types

commands.example: Navigate to 'Views' > 'File Types' > 'By MIME Type'
commands.explanation: Groups files by their detected internal signature or MIME type

commands.example: Navigate to 'Views' > 'Deleted Files' > 'File System'
commands.explanation: Shows files that have been marked as deleted but are still recoverable

commands.example: Click the 'Size' column header in the result viewer
commands.explanation: Orders the file list by size to identify unusually large or small files

commands.example: Enter a specific word in the Keyword Search bar and search
commands.explanation: Locates exact string matches within the indexed text of the case

commands.example: Enter a partial word and select 'Substring match'
commands.explanation: Finds words that contain the search term as a fragment

commands.example: Enter a regular expression like email patterns and search
commands.explanation: Uses patterns to find structured data like emails or credit card numbers

commands.example: Click 'Save' on a search result tab to persist findings
commands.explanation: Saves the current search results to the directory tree for reporting

commands.example: Click the 'Timeline' button in the top toolbar
commands.explanation: Launches the visualization tool for chronological event analysis

commands.example: Select 'Counts' mode in Timeline to see activity spikes
commands.explanation: Displays a bar chart of event frequency to identify periods of high activity

commands.example: Select 'Details' mode to see specific event descriptions
commands.explanation: Lists individual events like file creation or web access in chronological order

commands.example: Uncheck 'File System' to focus only on 'Web Activity'
commands.explanation: Reduces noise by hiding specific categories of temporal events

commands.example: Go to 'Tags' options and create a 'Malware Suspect' tag
commands.explanation: Defines a new label for categorizing evidence during analysis

commands.example: Right-click a file and select 'Add Tag' > 'Bookmark'
commands.explanation: Marks a specific file for later review or inclusion in the final report

commands.example: Right-click a Web History entry and tag it
commands.explanation: Marks a specific analysis result rather than the source file

commands.example: Click 'Generate Report', select 'HTML', and choose data
commands.explanation: Creates a browser-viewable report of selected artifacts and tags

commands.example: Select 'Excel' format in the Generate Report wizard
commands.explanation: Exports artifact tables to a spreadsheet for external manipulation

commands.example: Tools > Options to adjust memory usage and display settings
commands.explanation: Adjusts application-wide preferences for performance and view

commands.example: Tools > Options > Hash Sets to import a new MD5 database
commands.explanation: Adds external hash databases for filtering known files

commands.example: Tools > Options > Keyword Search to add custom lists
commands.explanation: Configures predefined lists of search terms for automated searching

commands.example: Right-click a folder and choose 'Run Ingest Modules'
commands.explanation: Executes analysis modules on a specific directory rather than the whole image

commands.example: Use the Ingest Progress monitor to cancel or reorder jobs
commands.explanation: Manages system resources by controlling which analysis tasks run first

commands.example: Review 'Shellbags' artifact for folder access history
commands.explanation: Reveals which folders were viewed by the user and when

commands.example: Review 'Prefetch' artifacts for application execution
commands.explanation: Determines which programs were run, how often, and when last executed

commands.example: Review 'Shimcache' for evidence of program execution compatibility
commands.explanation: Identifies applications present on the system even if deleted

commands.example: Check 'Recent Documents' artifact from registry/LNK files
commands.explanation: Lists documents recently opened by the user

commands.example: Browse extracted emails in the 'Email' artifact node
commands.explanation: Reads the body, headers, and attachments of processed emails

commands.example: Review 'Call Logs' artifact from mobile backups
commands.explanation: Lists incoming, outgoing, and missed calls extracted from mobile data

commands.example: Review 'Contacts' artifact for address book entries
commands.explanation: Displays contact names and numbers found in mobile or email data

commands.example: Review 'Messages' artifact for SMS/MMS content
commands.explanation: Reads short message service content extracted from phone dumps

commands.example: Enable 'PhotoRec Carver' in Ingest Modules
commands.explanation: Recovers deleted files from unallocated space by signature scanning

commands.example: Run the 'Strings' task or view the Strings tab
commands.explanation: Extracts printable characters from binary files to find hidden text

commands.example: Inspect file headers in the Hex view to verify file type
commands.explanation: Visually validates the file signature against the extension

commands.example: Browse the virtual 'Registry' folders in the tree view
commands.explanation: Navigates the Windows Registry hive structure manually

commands.example: Open the 'Geolocation' window to see points on a map
commands.explanation: Visualizes GPS coordinates extracted from images and metadata on a map

commands.example: Open the 'Communications' visualization tool
commands.explanation: Graphs relationships between accounts based on calls and emails

commands.example: Check 'Startup Items' artifact for persistence mechanisms
commands.explanation: Identifies programs configured to launch automatically at boot

commands.example: Keyword search for common webshell functions like 'eval' or 'base64_decode'
commands.explanation: Detects potential backdoors in web server directories

commands.example: Sort executables by entropy or check file type analysis
commands.explanation: Finds files with high entropy that may be packed malware

commands.example: Right-click a file and select 'Extract File(s)'
commands.explanation: Copies a file from the forensic image to the local file system

commands.example: Right-click a result table and 'Export to CSV'
commands.explanation: Saves the current list view as a CSV file for external analysis

commands.example: Reporting > Portable Case to create a shareable viewer
commands.explanation: Generates a standalone version of the case for non-technical stakeholders

commands.example: Options > Ingest Profiles to save a set of modules
commands.explanation: Saves a specific configuration of ingest modules for repeatable workflows

commands.example: Apply the 'Known Files' filter to hide NSRL matches
commands.explanation: Hides system files found in the whitelist to focus on user data

commands.example: Enable 'Android Analyzer' module on a phone dump
commands.explanation: Parses SQLite databases specific to Android apps and system

commands.example: Review 'GPS Trackpoints' artifact
commands.explanation: Examines route history data from navigation applications

commands.example: Open a .db file to view tables within Autopsy
commands.explanation: Browses the schema and content of SQLite databases directly in the content viewer

commands.example: Settings > Cuckoo to connect for malware detonation
commands.explanation: Links Autopsy to a Cuckoo instance for automated malware analysis

commands.example: Configure paths to run Volatility plugins on memory dumps
commands.explanation: Integrates memory forensics tools to analyze RAM captures

commands.example: Search for files with 'Creation Time' in a specific range
commands.explanation: Finds files based on metadata properties rather than content

commands.example: Enable the 'Credit Card' regex in Keyword Search
commands.explanation: Scans for sequences matching major credit card numbering standards

commands.example: Enable 'IP Address' regex in Keyword Search
commands.explanation: Scans for IPv4 and IPv6 address patterns in files and logs

commands.example: Check if critical system binaries have valid signatures
commands.explanation: Confirms that system files have not been modified by an attacker

commands.example: Parse Windows Event Logs for Event ID 4624 (Logon)
commands.explanation: Reviews authentication logs to identify unauthorized access attempts

commands.example: Keyword search for 'decrypt', 'bitcoin', or 'readme'
commands.explanation: Identifies presence of ransomware instructions

commands.example: Filter by size and extension .zip/.rar/.7z
commands.explanation: Finds potential data staging archives created for exfiltration

commands.example: Browse AppData/Local/Temp for executable files
commands.explanation: Inspects temporary folders often used as malware staging grounds

commands.example: Run a profile with only 'Recent Activity' and 'Hash Lookup'
commands.explanation: Performs a rapid analysis to get quick wins before full processing

commands.example: Configure PostgreSQL/SQLite central repo in settings
commands.explanation: Enables cross-case correlation of artifacts and hashes

commands.example: Mark an item as 'Notable' in the Central Repository
commands.explanation: Tags an artifact so it is flagged in all future cases

commands.example: Connect case to a multi-user Solr text index server
commands.explanation: Offloads text indexing to a dedicated server for collaborative cases

commands.example: Tools > Python Plugins to add community scripts
commands.explanation: Extends functionality with user-created Python ingest modules

commands.example: Tools > Plugins > Available Plugins to install .nbm files
commands.explanation: Adds features via the NetBeans Module format

commands.example: Add a comment to a specific file artifact
commands.explanation: Attaches examiner notes to a specific finding

commands.example: Use 'Other Occurrences' to see if file exists in other cases
commands.explanation: Checks the Central Repository to see if the file was seen before

commands.example: Use a YARA ingest plugin to scan for malware signatures
commands.explanation: Applies pattern matching rules to identify malware families

commands.example: Import Plaso storage file into Autopsy timeline
commands.explanation: Visualizes timelines generated by the Plaso super-timeline tool

commands.example: Analyze SAM registry hive for user details
commands.explanation: Extracts local user account information and group memberships

commands.example: Analyze SYSTEM registry hive for hardware/timezone info
commands.explanation: Extracts system-wide configuration and mounted device lists

commands.example: Analyze SOFTWARE hive for installed apps and OS version
commands.explanation: Extracts software configuration and operating system metadata

commands.example: Analyze NTUSER.DAT for user-specific settings
commands.explanation: Extracts user actions, search history, and recent file access

commands.example: Analyze Amcache.hve for program execution evidence
commands.explanation: Identifies executables that were run or present on the system

commands.example: Generate report in STIX format for threat intel
commands.explanation: Formats findings into Structured Threat Information Expression for sharing

commands.example: Generate Google Earth KML report from geolocation data
commands.explanation: Creates a map file compatible with Google Earth for location tracking

commands.example: Use image verification tool to check E01 hash
commands.explanation: Ensures the forensic image has not been corrupted since acquisition

commands.example: Run Autopsy as Admin and add 'Local Disk'
commands.explanation: Ingests a currently attached physical drive for analysis

commands.example: Edit carving rules to add new file signatures
commands.explanation: Defines custom headers and footers for recovering unsupported file types

commands.example: Use video triage module to capture frames
commands.explanation: Extracts still images from video files for quick review

commands.example: Select 'Images' in the tree to view thumbnail grid
commands.explanation: Displays all found images in a gallery layout for rapid visual triage

commands.example: Sort email artifacts by 'From' address
commands.explanation: Isolates communications originating from a specific suspect

commands.example: Apply date filter to email messages
commands.explanation: Focuses email review on the relevant timeframe of the incident

commands.example: Review 'Scheduled Tasks' artifacts (.job/.xml)
commands.explanation: Checks for automated tasks often used for malware persistence

commands.example: Review System registry for service configurations
commands.explanation: Identifies installed background services and their startup types

commands.example: Search for RDP logs or PsExec artifacts
commands.explanation: Finds evidence of an attacker moving between systems in the network

commands.example: Analyze large file transfers or cloud storage sync logs
commands.explanation: Identifies data leaving the network via upload or external drive

commands.example: View 'ConsoleHost_history.txt' or PSReadLine artifacts
commands.explanation: Reviews executed PowerShell commands for malicious scripts

commands.example: View '.bash_history' files on Linux/Mac images
commands.explanation: Reviews command line history on Unix-like systems

commands.example: Review 'Web Form Autofill' artifacts
commands.explanation: Recovers text typed into web forms like search bars or login fields

commands.example: Review 'Web Cache' artifacts for cached images/scripts
commands.explanation: Examines stored web content to reconstruct page views

commands.example: Manually delete the case directory via OS
commands.explanation: Removes the case database and reports to free up disk space

commands.example: Select a case from the 'Recent Cases' list on startup
commands.explanation: Quickly resumes work on a previously accessed investigation

commands.example: Case > Close Case to release locks
commands.explanation: Safely closes the database connection and saves the current state

commands.example: Look for installed Dropbox/OneDrive databases
commands.explanation: Identifies synced files and account information for cloud services

commands.example: Identify partitions with BitLocker signatures
commands.explanation: Recognizes volumes encrypted with Windows native encryption

commands.example: Identify high-entropy files or mounted volume artifacts
commands.explanation: Flags potential encrypted containers used to hide data

commands.example: Add VSS snapshots as data sources
commands.explanation: Accesses historical versions of files from Windows Restore points

commands.example: Compare file differences between Shadow Copies
commands.explanation: Identifies what changed on the system between two points in time

commands.example: Import drone flight logs (DAT/TXT)
commands.explanation: Parses flight paths and telemetry from UAV devices

commands.example: Use 'Hide Known Files' option in views
commands.explanation: Suppresses files matching the NSRL whitelist from the view

commands.example: Toggle 'Group by Data Source' in the tree
commands.explanation: Separates artifacts based on which disk image they came from

commands.example: Drag the Data Content viewer to a second monitor
commands.explanation: Customizes the workspace layout for multi-monitor setups

commands.example: Backup the case database file (autopsy.db)
commands.explanation: Creates a manual backup of the investigation progress

commands.example: Review parsed Shortcut files
commands.explanation: Determines target paths and timestamps of accessed files/folders

commands.example: Review Automatic/Custom Jump List artifacts
commands.explanation: Shows recent files accessed via the Taskbar or Start Menu

commands.example: Review thumbnail cache database entries
commands.explanation: Views thumbnails of images that may have been deleted

commands.example: Parse the ActivitiesCache.db
commands.explanation: Reconstructs user activity history across devices

commands.example: Parse SRUDB.dat for network usage
commands.explanation: Analyzes System Resource Usage Monitor for app network/CPU history

commands.example: Ingest a .pcap file as a logical file
commands.explanation: Adds network capture files for basic extraction (requires plugins for deep analysis)

commands.example: Use Regex for 'http://' and 'https://'
commands.explanation: Extracts all web links found in files and unallocated space

commands.example: Use Regex for email pattern
commands.explanation: Harvests all email addresses found on the disk

commands.example: Use Regex for various phone number formats
commands.explanation: Identifies potential contact numbers in documents and logs

commands.example: Execute a custom .py script via the Python Console
commands.explanation: Runs automated tasks using the internal Java-Python environment

commands.example: Review 'Extension Mismatch Detected' results
commands.explanation: Investigates files where the header does not match the file extension

commands.example: View compile time and sections of .exe files
commands.explanation: Analyzes executable structure to find anomalies or spoofing

commands.example: View entropy score in file metadata
commands.explanation: Measures randomness to detect compression or encryption in malware

commands.example: Generate report containing ONLY tagged items
commands.explanation: Creates a concise report focused only on pertinent evidence

commands.example: Export timeline events to CSV
commands.explanation: Creates a chronological list of events for external timeline tools

commands.example: Update Solr configuration for new index fields
commands.explanation: Modifies the search index structure (Advanced Admin task)

commands.example: Manually clear temp image cache if disk is full
commands.explanation: Frees up space used by cached thumbnails and temporary files

commands.example: Help > Open Log Folder to view autopsy.log
commands.explanation: Troubleshoots errors that occurred during the ingest process

commands.example: Correlate 'Downloads' folder with browser history
commands.explanation: Confirms if a file on disk originated from a specific web download

commands.example: Parse $Recycle.Bin artifacts
commands.explanation: Identifies who deleted a file and when it was deleted

commands.example: Check if an imported hash set is responding
commands.explanation: Ensures the known-good/bad filters are active and accurate

commands.example: Search for hex byte sequence in Hex View
commands.explanation: Finds specific binary signatures manually within a file

commands.example: Right-click 'Unallocated Space' > Extract
commands.explanation: Exports raw unallocated data for external carving tools

commands.example: Run Autopsy from USB on suspect machine (Lite Mode)
commands.explanation: Performs data collection on a live system (requires portable setup)

commands.example: Analyze RDP bitmap cache artifacts
commands.explanation: Reconstructs visual fragments of previous Remote Desktop sessions

commands.example: Keyword search for 'TeamViewer' logs
commands.explanation: Identifies remote access sessions via TeamViewer software

commands.example: Locate AnyDesk 'ad.trace' files
commands.explanation: Analyzes logs for AnyDesk remote connection history

commands.example: Tools > Options > Keyword Search > Rebuild
commands.explanation: Re-indexes the case text if search results are corrupted

commands.example: Filter for Event ID 7045
commands.explanation: Detects when a new service was installed (common malware persistence)

commands.example: Filter for Event ID 4688
commands.explanation: Tracks which processes were launched and by whom

commands.example: Filter for Event ID 4720
commands.explanation: Identifies when a new user account was added to the system

commands.example: Filter for Event ID 1102
commands.explanation: Detects if the audit log was manually cleared to hide tracks

commands.example: Export file listing with MAC times to CSV
commands.explanation: Generates a file system bodyfile for external timeline analysis

commands.example: Compress the entire case directory
commands.explanation: Packages the case data and reports for long-term cold storage

commands.example: Check generated report files against findings
commands.explanation: Quality assurance step to ensure report accurately reflects evidence
